Fern Polrrrcal PRACTTcEs C ovrrr,rrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April 18,20A7
John J. Ruprecht
Attorney atLaw
P O Box 1445
Fort Bragg, Califomia 95437
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-07-066
This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 pleasl noteihat our
advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the
application of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict oiinterest or
Govemment Code Section 1090. We also offerno opinion on the application ofHealth
and Safety code section32l2L 3 to Government code section 109b.
The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to anyperson whose
duties under the Act are in question. (section g311a@); Regulation tni6@1, copy
enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written aavice'if it would
be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interesfto do so. (Regulation
18329_(b)(8XF).) Formal written advice is the application of law io igi't "r, 
set of facts
provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor.
(Section 83114; Regulation 15329(b)(7).) The facts statedln yor.leiter imply two
questions.
We decline to provide written advice regarding the propriety of Dr. Kermen
having entered into a contract with the Mendocino Coast Healih Care District. This
question involves activities that have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would
relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective
administration of the Act mandated by Section 83111. (See Regulation 1g329(bXgXA).)
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request.
' Gov"*m"rrt Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sectiors
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
@.1


File No. W-07-066
Page No. 2
With respect to the question of whether Dr. Kerrnen has a conflict of interest as a
board member of the district while under contract with the district, the Act,s g"n"rul
conflict-of-interest rule is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official
makes a govemmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable mateialfinancial efftct
on one or more of his or her financial interests. (Government Code Section g7100.)you
have not provided facts regardlng any govefirmental decisions coming before the board
thatmay affect Dr. Kermen's financial interests. Accordingly, we cainot provide further
guidance at this time. If a particular governmental decision Jould come ,!, yor, *uy
wish to write to us at that time.
For a ganeral discussion of the application of the Act's conflict-of-interest
provisions we are enclosing a copy of the commission's fact sheet entitled ..can I vote?
Conflicts of Interest Overview.,,
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
vJjgl
